serving.description: 1 serving (approximately 3–4 köfte patties with sliced onions)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of izgara lamb köfte consists of approximately 3 to 4 grilled minced lamb patties (roughly 250 g total) garnished with sliced raw onions and often accompanied by flatbread or rice.

nutrient_source.title: Lamb, ground, cooked, broiled — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 17066)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Izgara lamb köfte is prepared from seasoned ground lamb shaped into patties and grilled. USDA SR Legacy 'Lamb, ground, cooked, broiled' (NDB 17066) is the closest approved match for the cooked minced lamb base. Values represent the cooked lamb component per 100 g. Onion garnish and spices contribute negligible macronutrient change at typical proportions and are not separately accounted for in per-100g values.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure meat and meat preparations containing negligible carbohydrate (carbohydrate_g = 0.0 per 100 g) are assigned a glycemic index of 1 (effectively zero) by convention in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI literature, as they elicit no measurable postprandial glucose response. Izgara lamb köfte contains no glycemic carbohydrates; glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this food contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention; GL per serving is 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~92 mg per 100 g); no added high-sodium sauces in the base preparation
hypertension.guidance: Izgara lamb köfte has a relatively low sodium content in its plain grilled form; individuals managing hypertension should monitor portion size and avoid high-sodium accompaniments such as pickled vegetables or salty dips.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content; high protein supports satiety and glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: With effectively zero glycaemic carbohydrates and a high protein content of approximately 25.6 g per 100 g, izgara lamb köfte has a minimal impact on postprandial blood glucose and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-carbohydrate accompaniments such as white rice or flatbread.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (~6.9 g per 100 g) and dietary cholesterol (~97 mg per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat and cholesterol content of grilled lamb köfte are clinically relevant for individuals with cardiovascular disease; portion control is advised and the dish should be consumed as part of a diet rich in vegetables, legumes, and unsaturated fats in line with heart-healthy dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Izgara Lamb Köfte (also romanized as Izgara Kuzu Köfte) is a classic Turkish grilled meat dish consisting of seasoned minced lamb shaped into elongated or round patties and cooked directly over a charcoal or gas grill. The word 'izgara' means 'grilled' in Turkish, and 'köfte' refers to the spiced minced-meat preparation common across Turkish, Middle Eastern, and Central Asian cuisines. The lamb mince is typically seasoned with onion, parsley, cumin, black pepper, and paprika before being hand-formed and grilled until charred on the outside and juicy within. In Singapore, this dish is served at Turkish and Lebanese restaurants such as Sultan Palace at Boat Quay, where it is presented with sliced raw onions and may be accompanied by flatbread, rice pilaf, or a simple salad. Nutritionally, izgara lamb köfte is a high-protein, low-carbohydrate food providing approximately 258 kcal, 25.6 g protein, and 16.7 g fat per 100 g. The fat profile includes saturated fat (~6.9 g) and moderate monounsaturated fat (~7.0 g). Carbohydrate content is negligible, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate and diabetic dietary patterns. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free in its base form. It is a good source of zinc, vitamin B12, niacin, and selenium, reflecting the nutritional density of lamb as a red meat. Clinically, the moderate saturated fat content warrants portion awareness for individuals managing cardiovascular risk, while the low glycaemic impact makes it appropriate for blood glucose management.
common_user_queries: Is izgara lamb good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Turkish izgara lamb köfte exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Lamb, ground, cooked, broiled' (NDB 17066), which is the closest approved source match for the cooked minced lamb base. The adaptation is defensible given the preparation method (grilling of seasoned ground lamb) but introduces moderate uncertainty, particularly for sodium and micronutrients that may vary with seasoning.
notes: 1. carbohydrate_g is 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy data for cooked ground lamb; this is confirmed zero as pure cooked minced lamb contains no glycaemic carbohydrates. sugar_g is 0.0 confirmed zero for the same reason. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 confirmed zero as there is no plant fibre in the cooked lamb base. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 confirmed zero for cooked lamb. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for cooked ground lamb. 2. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this food contains no glycaemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low by convention per University of Sydney GI Database methodology for zero-carbohydrate meat foods; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked ground lamb (NDB 17066). vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked ground lamb (NDB 17066). 4. The food name in the input record is listed as '51. Izgera Lamb' (menu numbering and variant spelling); the canonical English name has been corrected to 'Izgara Lamb Köfte' reflecting the standard Turkish culinary term. 5. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant context (Sultan Palace Turkish and Lebanese Cuisine); this has not been independently verified against a halal certification body. 6. Serving size of 250 g is estimated based on typical Turkish restaurant portion sizes for a main-course köfte plate; no official serving size data is available for this specific menu item. 7. FoodOn URI FOODON_03302051 and FoodEx2 code A0CKP are used as closest-parent anchors; no exact ontology codes exist for Turkish grilled lamb köfte.
